title: Image/GIF button popover, send/voice button swap in chat input

requirements.summary: Move image/GIF button inside chat input field with popover overlay for image upload and GIF picker
acceptanceCriteria: Image/GIF button is rendered inside the chat input container on the left side
Clicking the button opens a floating popover/modal panel positioned above the chat input
The popover contains image upload and GIF search/picker options (matching existing functionality in MessageInput.tsx)
Clicking outside the popover or pressing Escape closes it
The popover does not use a dropdown pattern

file_operations.description: Move the image/GIF button from its current position into the left side of the chat input field. Add state to control popover visibility. Implement a floating popover/modal overlay component that anchors above the chat input and contains both the image upload interface (file input with preview) and the Giphy GIF picker (search input, trending GIFs, search results). The popover must close on outside clicks and Escape key press. Preserve all existing image upload and Giphy functionality (compression, progress tracking, result rendering). Use absolute positioning or a portal to render the popover above the input without obstructing the message list.

requirements.summary: Show voice button by default, swap to send button when input is active or has text
acceptanceCriteria: Voice message button is visible by default when the chat input is not focused and empty
Send button is hidden by default
When the chat input is focused, the voice button is hidden and the send button appears in the same position
When the input loses focus and contains no text, the send button hides and the voice button reappears
If the input has text and loses focus, the send button remains visible
The swap is visually smooth (e.g., transition or instant swap)

file_operations.description: Implement conditional rendering logic to swap between the voice message button and the send button on the right side of the chat input. Add state to track whether the input is focused and whether it contains text. Show the voice button by default. When the input gains focus or has non-empty text, hide the voice button and show the send button in the same position. When the input loses focus and is empty, revert to showing the voice button. Ensure both buttons occupy the same visual space and the transition is smooth (either instant swap or CSS transition). Preserve all existing voice recording and message sending functionality.
